div1.entryFree: Veil, vb. to cover with a veil or curtain: Merch. III, 2, 99. Tw. I, 1, 28. Cor. II, 1, 231. Metaphorically, to conceal, to disguise: and --ed in them, did win whom he would maim, Compl. 312. to keep your great pretences --ed, Cor. I, 2, 20. if I have --ed my look, Caes. I, 2, 37. In Meas. IV, 6, 4 O. Edd. to vail full purpose; some M. Edd. to v. full p.; others to vailful p.
